title: SAT Reading & Writing
description: Digital SAT Reading & Writing: comprehension, grammar, and effective expression across difficulty levels.

questions.passage: The octopus is among the most intelligent invertebrates. In laboratory settings, octopuses have learned to open jars, navigate mazes, and even recognize individual human handlers — feats once thought to require a backboned brain.
questions.prompt: The main purpose of the passage is to:

choices.text: highlight the surprising intelligence of octopuses
argue that octopuses should not be kept in labs

questions.explanation: The passage lists cognitive feats to emphasize that octopuses are surprisingly intelligent for invertebrates.

questions.prompt: Choose the option that makes the sentence correct: "Each of the students ______ responsible for their own project."
questions.explanation: "Each" is singular, so it takes the singular verb "is."

questions.prompt: Which choice best combines the sentences? "The experiment failed. The researchers learned a great deal from it."

choices.text: The experiment failed, the researchers learned a great deal from it.
The experiment failed, but the researchers learned a great deal from it.
The experiment failed the researchers learned a great deal from it.
The experiment failed; and the researchers learned a great deal from it.

questions.explanation: A comma plus the coordinating conjunction "but" correctly joins two independent clauses and signals contrast. Option A is a comma splice.

choices.text: Due to the fact that it was raining, we stayed inside.
Because it was raining, we stayed inside.
On account of the rain that was falling, we stayed inside.
It was raining, and for that reason we stayed inside.

questions.explanation: "Because" conveys the cause-and-effect meaning most concisely.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct word: "Neither of the boys finished ______ homework."
questions.explanation: "Neither" is singular, so it pairs with the singular "his" here.

questions.prompt: Choose the best transition: "The new policy reduced costs; ______, it angered many employees."
questions.explanation: The two clauses contrast (a benefit vs. a downside), so the contrasting transition "however" fits.

choices.text: She brought three things: a map, a compass, and water.
She brought three things; a map, a compass, and water.
She brought three things, a map, a compass, and water.
She brought: three things a map, a compass, and water.

questions.explanation: A colon correctly introduces a list after a complete independent clause.

questions.passage: Urban beekeeping has surged in popularity over the past decade. Advocates argue that city hives boost pollination of community gardens. Yet some ecologists caution that a glut of honeybees may crowd out native wild bees, which are often more effective pollinators.
questions.prompt: The passage suggests that the ecologists' main concern is that urban beekeeping may:

choices.text: increase the cost of beekeeping equipment

questions.explanation: The ecologists caution that honeybees "may crowd out native wild bees" — concern for native bee populations.

questions.passage: Critics initially dismissed the painter's late works as the careless output of failing eyesight. Recent conservation, however, has revealed deliberate underdrawing beneath the loose brushwork, suggesting that the apparent looseness was a chosen style rather than a symptom of decline.
questions.prompt: The passage implies that the recent conservation findings:

choices.text: challenge the view that the late style resulted from declining eyesight
prove the painter could no longer see clearly
show the paintings were made by an assistant

questions.explanation: Deliberate underdrawing implies intentional technique, undercutting the claim that the loose style was merely caused by failing eyesight.

questions.prompt: Which word most precisely completes the sentence? "The committee's report was so ______ that readers could draw opposite conclusions from the same paragraph."
questions.explanation: If opposite conclusions can be drawn from the same text, it is open to multiple interpretations — "ambiguous."
